task_completion.description: Open the 'Fluorescent Cells' sample image in ImageJ/Fiji.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The ImageJ/Fiji application is open, displaying an image window titled 'Fluorescent Cells' (or similar) showing biological cells with red, green, and blue channels.

task_completion.description: Change the Red channel (Channel 1) to Magenta while keeping the Green channel (Channel 2) Green.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The previously red structures in the image are now colored magenta. The 'Channels' tool or LUT menu may be visible showing 'Magenta' selected for Channel 1, and 'Green' for Channel 2.

task_completion.description: Set the spatial calibration to 0.16 µm/pixel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Set Scale' dialog box is visible with parameters reflecting 0.16 µm/pixel (e.g., Distance in pixels = 1, Known distance = 0.16, Unit of length = micron/um), OR the image window header updates to show the new physical dimensions/scale.

task_completion.description: Add a 10 µm white scale bar in the lower right corner.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A solid white horizontal line (scale bar) is visible in the lower right quadrant of the image. The 'Scale Bar' dialog box may be visible with 'Width in microns' set to 10, 'Color' set to White, and 'Location' set to Lower Right.

task_completion.description: Save the final flattened RGB image as a PNG to ~/ImageJ_Data/results/accessible_figure.png.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog box, file explorer, or terminal shows the image being saved with the filename 'accessible_figure.png' in the '/home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/' directory. The image type should be selected as PNG.

integrity.description: The agent used the ImageJ/Fiji GUI to perform the image manipulations.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show active interaction with ImageJ/Fiji menus (e.g., Image > Color, Analyze > Set Scale, Analyze > Tools > Scale Bar) and dialog boxes, rather than using terminal commands like ImageMagick to process the image.

integrity.description: The scale bar was generated using the software's built-in tool rather than drawn manually.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 'Scale Bar' dialog box from ImageJ/Fiji should be visible at some point, ensuring the scale bar is mathematically accurate based on the calibration, rather than a manually drawn white rectangle using a basic paint tool.

integrity.description: The agent navigated to the correct target directory to save the file.
integrity.visual_evidence: The save dialog or file manager explicitly shows the path '/home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/' being accessed or created, rather than saving to a default desktop or home folder.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Output path: /home/ga/ImageJ_Data/results/accessible_figure.png. Spatial calibration must be set to 0.16 microns per pixel. The scale bar must be 10 micrometers wide, white, and located in the lower right corner. Channel 1 (originally red) must be remapped to Magenta, while Channel 2 remains Green. The final image must not contain pure red pixels, and the white scale bar must be detectable in the bottom-right quadrant (>50 white pixels).
